1	# Tool Registry Foundation TechSpec
2	
3	## Executive Summary
4	
5	This TechSpec designs AGH's Tool Registry as a daemon-owned runtime service, not as a static list of built-in commands. The registry will unify tool identity, discovery, availability, policy, execution, hooks, telemetry, extension descriptors, MCP adapters, and session-visible exposure through one central dispatch pipeline.
6	
7	There is no existing `_prd.md` for this task. The scope is based on the user request, competitor research under `.compozy/tasks/tools-registry/analysis/`, accepted ADRs under `.compozy/tasks/tools-registry/adrs/`, current AGH code exploration, and the prior autonomous skills/tools registry gap analysis.
8	
9	The primary architectural trade-off is to make AGH-native tools visible to sessions through an AGH-hosted local MCP server in the MVP instead of trying to invent an ACP-specific registry. ACP does not define a callable tool registry; it defines session lifecycle, MCP bootstrap, permission callbacks, and tool-call observations. MCP provides the programmatic `Tool.name`, so AGH will expose its canonical `ToolID` directly as the hosted MCP tool name.
10	
11	The foundation will support executable native/bundled tools, executable extension-host tools, and executable MCP-backed tools. Built-in AGH tools execute in-process through `native_go` handles compiled into the daemon. Third-party TypeScript and Go extension tools execute out-of-process through the existing extension runtime, a new `tool.provider` capability, `provide_tools` reconciliation, and `tools/call` RPC. MCP-backed tools execute through daemon-owned MCP clients that consume the existing MCP config/auth subsystem. Descriptor-only is an unavailable/error state, not the MVP contract for extension or MCP tools.
12	
13	Implementation direction: AGH should use `github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go` for MCP protocol/session/transport behavior instead of planning hand-rolled MCP JSON-RPC, stdio, SSE, or streamable-HTTP plumbing inside the Tool Registry workstream. AGH still owns registry policy, canonical `ToolID`, approval bridging, UDS/session binding, provenance, redaction, and durable MCP auth state.
14	
15	## MVP Boundary Statement
16	
17	MVP boundary: implementation steps 1-16 build the Tool Registry foundation, AGH-hosted MCP session exposure, native bootstrap tools, executable TypeScript/Go extension-host tools, executable daemon-owned MCP call-through, shared CLI/HTTP/UDS surfaces, policy/availability enforcement, hooks, observability, docs, SDK updates, and verification. This MVP proves the registry as an executable daemon primitive without replacing every ACP provider-native tool.
18	
19	Post-MVP work deferred to later TechSpecs:
20	
21	- direct driver-specific tool injection outside hosted MCP;
22	- full shell/browser/file tool replacement for ACP runtimes;
23	- remote peer tool execution over AGH Network;
24	- provider-specific deferred schema loading such as Anthropic `tool_reference`;
25	- broad marketplace signing/trust overhaul;
26	- skill install/remove/update tools;
27	- bridge SDK executable tool adapters;
28	- direct in-process plugin loading for third-party Go or TypeScript code;
29	- client-supplied ACP `mcpServers` as session-scoped registry sources.
30	
31	Explicitly out of scope for this TechSpec:
32	
33	- in-process third-party extension handlers;
34	- silent compatibility aliases for dotted tool IDs;
35	- policy bypasses for `approve-all`;
36	- partial surface delivery where CLI/HTTP ships without UDS, docs, codegen, and hosted MCP parity;
37	- storing matchable ownership or authorization state inside opaque JSON metadata blobs.
38	
39	Backend delivery boundary:
40	
41	| Backend kind | MVP delivery | Invocation behavior |
42	|---|---|---|
43	| `native_go` | Descriptor, availability, policy, and full dispatch through `Registry.Call` | Executable in-process only for daemon-compiled AGH built-ins |
44	| `extension_host` | Manifest-authoritative descriptor, runtime reconciliation, extension health, source policy, and full dispatch through `Registry.Call` | Executable out-of-process through existing extension subprocess runtime, `tool.provider`, and `tools/call`; TypeScript and Go SDKs wrap handlers as functions |
45	| `mcp` | Descriptor discovery, source provenance, health/auth diagnostics, collision handling, source policy, and full dispatch through `Registry.Call` | Executable through daemon-owned MCP clients using existing MCP config and `internal/mcp/auth` status/token interfaces |
46	| `subprocess` | Not a public registry backend kind in MVP | Rejected by manifest validation; third-party Go/TypeScript tools use `extension_host`, whose implementation is subprocess-isolated |
47	| `bridge` | Reserved post-MVP backend kind | Rejected by MVP validation unless a later bridge TechSpec enables it |
48	
49	AGH-hosted MCP is different from `mcp` backend tools. Hosted MCP is the session exposure transport for AGH registry tools; `mcp` backend tools are external tools contributed by MCP servers and are executable only through daemon-owned MCP client adapters after the same registry policy, source, approval, hook, and session-lineage gates pass.
50	
51	## Architectural Boundaries
52	
53	`internal/daemon` remains the only composition root. It may import and compose `internal/tools`, `internal/skills`, `internal/mcp`, `internal/extension`, `internal/hooks`, `internal/session`, `internal/network`, `internal/task`, `internal/api/*`, and config/resource stores. No package may import `internal/daemon`.
54	
55	Package import boundaries:
56	
57	- `internal/tools` owns `ToolID`, descriptors, backend kinds, providers, handles, registry, policy interfaces, availability, dispatch contracts, and result normalization. It must not import `internal/daemon`, `internal/api/*`, `internal/cli`, `internal/extension`, `internal/session`, `internal/network`, or `internal/task`.
58	- `internal/catalog`, if added, is a thin composition-facing facade over `internal/tools` and `internal/skills`. It must not own tool dispatch or policy.
59	- `internal/extension` may publish manifest-authoritative tool descriptors and expose live out-of-process extension tool invokers through public registry contracts. It must not execute third-party tool handlers in-process and must not import registry internals beyond public `internal/tools` descriptor/provider contracts.
60	- `internal/mcp` may adapt external MCP tools, call external MCP servers through daemon-owned clients, and host the AGH MCP stdio proxy. It wraps `mark3labs/mcp-go` behind AGH-owned interfaces and must keep all AGH-owned calls entering `internal/tools.Registry.Call` through UDS or an injected interface; MCP code must not duplicate dispatch policy.
61	- `internal/mcp/auth` already owns remote MCP OAuth 2.1 + PKCE, redacted status, token refresh/logout, and durable token storage through `internal/store/globaldb`. The Tool Registry may consume redacted auth status through a daemon-injected interface, but it must not reimplement OAuth flows, open the MCP auth token store directly, or persist remote MCP token material.
62	- `internal/api/core` owns transport-independent handlers. `internal/api/httpapi` and `internal/api/udsapi` only register routes and transport concerns.
63	- `internal/cli` calls UDS/HTTP client methods and does not import runtime registry implementations.
64	- `internal/hooks` owns typed hook payloads and execution. Hooks dispatch at the registry call site; no code may tail event tables to trigger tool hooks.
65	- `internal/store` may persist session lineage permission atoms and events. It must not decide tool policy; it validates and stores normalized atoms.
66	- `internal/session` can receive hosted MCP server config and session projections through interfaces. It must not implement a parallel tool registry.
67	
68	Boundaries to update in implementation:
69	
70	- If `internal/catalog` or a new `internal/mcp` subpackage is added, update `magefile.go` package boundary checks in the same change.
71	- Any OpenAPI/contract change must co-ship generated `openapi/agh.json` and `web/src/generated/agh-openapi.d.ts`.
72	- Any CLI surface must co-ship HTTP/UDS parity unless the spec explicitly marks the surface CLI-only. This TechSpec has no CLI-only tool surfaces.
73	
74	## System Architecture
75	
76	### Component Overview
77	
78	| Component | Responsibility | Boundary |
79	|---|---|---|
80	| `internal/tools` runtime registry | Owns canonical `ToolID`, descriptors, backend kinds, providers, handles, availability projection, policy evaluation, dispatch, result normalization, and telemetry hooks | Does not import `daemon`, `api`, or `cli`; composed by `internal/daemon` |
81	| Cold `tool` resources | Persist desired-state tool metadata from extensions, bundles, and future dynamic producers | Metadata only; no function pointers or execution handles |
82	| Built-in `native_go` provider | Registers AGH-native tools such as tool search, skill view, network peers/send, and bounded task tools | In-process only because it ships inside the daemon binary |
83	| Extension-host provider | Converts extension-published tool resources into runtime descriptors, reconciles them with `provide_tools`, and invokes handlers over extension subprocess RPC | Does not execute extension code in-process; fails closed on manifest/runtime mismatch |
84	| Extension SDKs | TypeScript and Go helper APIs let extension authors define tools with functions while the runtime remains out-of-process | SDKs generate/reconcile manifest descriptors and register `tools/call` handlers |
85	| MCP adapter provider | Normalizes MCP-backed descriptors, health, auth status, source provenance, and executes calls through daemon-owned MCP clients wrapping `mark3labs/mcp-go` | Fails closed on health, auth, policy, approval, hook, or name collision problems |
86	| Existing MCP auth runtime | Supplies redacted remote MCP OAuth 2.1 + PKCE status for external MCP diagnostics | Owned by `internal/mcp/auth`; registry must not duplicate OAuth flow, token storage, or token refresh/logout |
87	| `internal/catalog` facade | Thin cross-domain list/search/view facade over tools and skills | Optional coordination layer; runtime tool dispatch remains in `internal/tools` |
88	| Policy engine | Combines ACP approval mode, session lineage, agent policy, source/risk defaults, registry allow/deny, toolsets, availability, and hooks | Produces structured effective decisions, never a single ambiguous boolean |
89	| AGH-hosted MCP proxy | Exposes session-callable AGH tools as MCP tools using canonical `ToolID` names | Runs through daemon-provided `agh tool mcp --session <id> --bind-nonce <nonce>`, uses `mark3labs/mcp-go` server/tool APIs over stdio, and proxies to daemon over UDS |
90	| API/CLI surfaces | Expose machine-readable list/search/info/invoke/status behavior | Shared contracts in `internal/api/contract`, handlers in `internal/api/core`, HTTP/UDS parity |
91	| Hook integration | Runs `tool.pre_call`, `tool.post_call`, and `tool.post_error` around registry dispatch | Hooks can deny, narrow, patch, redact, or annotate, but cannot bypass policy |
92	| Observability | Emits durable events and metrics for registration, projection, decisions, calls, failures, conflicts, truncation, and policy denials | Redacts secrets and raw tokens |
93	
94	Data flow:
95	
96	1. Extensions, built-ins, MCP servers, and future dynamic providers register cold descriptors and, where executable, runtime handles.
97	2. `internal/daemon` composes providers into `internal/tools.Registry`.
98	3. Registry indexes descriptors by canonical `ToolID`.
99	4. Operator surfaces can list all tools with status and reason codes.
100	5. Session/model-visible surfaces request a session projection and receive only callable tools for that effective context.
101	6. Every invocation enters `Registry.Call`, which validates schema, recomputes availability/policy, runs hooks, calls the `native_go`, `extension_host`, or `mcp` handle, normalizes output, persists/observes, and returns a bounded result.
102	
103	## Implementation Design
104	
105	### Core Interfaces
106

163	`MCPCallExecutor` is implemented by `internal/mcp`, not by `internal/tools`. It resolves bearer material through `internal/mcp/auth` internally, never exposes `mcpauth.TokenRecord` or raw headers to registry code, and returns only normalized `ToolResult` plus wrapped backend errors.
164	
165	### MCP Library Adoption
166	
167	`internal/mcp` wraps `github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go` for MCP protocol/session/transport behavior. This is a protocol implementation choice, not a policy boundary change.
168	
169	- MVP validation is pinned to `github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go v0.49.0`. Any library version upgrade requires fresh focused tests plus TechSpec/ADR review before adoption.
170	- Primary-source anchor points for this decision:
171	  - `README.md` and `www/docs/pages/transports/` document first-class `stdio`, `SSE`, and `streamable HTTP` support.
172	  - `mcp/tools.go` defines `Tool`, `RawInputSchema`, and `RawOutputSchema`, which align with AGH's descriptor-authored hosted schemas.
173	  - `server/server.go`, `server/session.go`, and `server/streamable_http.go` define hosted/server lifecycle, per-session tools, and `tools/list_changed` behavior.
174	  - `client/stdio.go`, `client/http.go`, and `client/sse.go` define the remote stdio, streamable HTTP, and SSE client paths.
175	- Hosted AGH MCP proxy uses `server.NewMCPServer`, explicit `mcp.Tool` definitions, and `server.ServeStdio`. AGH session projections still decide which tools are registered, and every tool call still re-enters `Registry.Call` through AGH-owned UDS/session binding.
176	- External stdio MCP servers use `client.NewStdioMCPClient`.
177	- External remote HTTP MCP servers use `client.NewStreamableHttpClient`.
178	- External remote SSE MCP servers use `client.NewSSEMCPClient`.
179	- Hosted MCP tool registration MUST construct `mcp.Tool` values with `RawInputSchema` and `RawOutputSchema` taken byte-for-byte from `Descriptor.input_schema` and `Descriptor.output_schema`. `WithInputSchema`, `WithOutputSchema`, and other reflection-based schema helpers are forbidden for AGH-hosted tools because AGH manifest/runtime digests treat descriptor schema bytes as authoritative.
180	- AGH keeps its stricter canonical `ToolID` grammar even though `mcp-go` accepts a broader MCP tool-name grammar. AGH `ToolID` remains a valid subset and is passed to the library as MCP `Tool.name`.
181	- Hosted MCP tool registration should use library list-change behavior rather than inventing a parallel MCP notification format. AGH still owns when a projection changes and which session is allowed to observe it.
182	- MVP does not rely on `WithContinuousListening()` or any long-lived upstream notification subscription from external MCP servers. Remote MCP sessions are on-demand, bounded by executor idle TTL, and use upstream `notifications/tools/list_changed` only as cache invalidation hints if they appear during an active session.
183	- AGH preserves the existing remote MCP config surface: `transport = "stdio" | "http" | "sse"`. `http` means streamable HTTP via `mcp-go`; `sse` means the library's explicit SSE client path. AGH must not silently rewrite one transport into another.
184	
185	Remote auth integration contract:
186	
187	- `internal/mcp/auth` remains the only authority for remote MCP login, refresh, logout, durable token storage, and redacted status.
188	- For remote HTTP and SSE requests, `internal/mcp` injects outbound headers from current AGH-owned credential state using library header hooks such as `WithHTTPHeaderFunc` and `WithHeaderFunc`. Raw access and refresh tokens never leave `internal/mcp`.
189	- Before a remote `tools/list` or `tools/call`, the executor inspects AGH's persisted auth state. If the state is `authenticated` or `expired` and the token is refreshable but no longer usable, the executor may attempt exactly one `internal/mcp/auth.Service.Refresh` before creating or retrying the outbound client request.
190	- If a remote transport still returns an auth failure after that bounded refresh path, the executor returns only redacted backend errors mapped to `mcp_auth_required`, `mcp_auth_expired`, `mcp_auth_invalid`, or `mcp_auth_refresh_failed`.
191	- AGH MUST NOT use `client.NewOAuthStreamableHttpClient`, `client.NewOAuthSSEClient`, default `transport.NewOAuthHandler`, `MemoryTokenStore`, or any library-owned login/cache/refresh flow as the authority for remote MCP credentials.
192	- If a later design wants to use `mcp-go` OAuth helper types for transport convenience, it must do so only behind an AGH-owned `TokenStore` adapter and a follow-up ADR. MVP does not depend on that path.

300	### Data Models
301	
302	`ToolID`
303	
304	- Canonical public ID used by registry, policy, CLI, HTTP, UDS, hooks, telemetry, and hosted MCP.
305	- Format: `<segment>( "__" <segment> )*`
306	- Segment regex: `[a-z][a-z0-9_]*`
307	- Max length: 64.
308	- Lowercase ASCII only.
309	- `__` is reserved as namespace separator.
310	- No dots, hyphens, uppercase, empty segments, or dual wire aliases.
311	- External MCP/extension names that sanitize to more than 64 characters are rejected as conflicted with `id_too_long`; AGH does not truncate, hash-suffix, or create shadow aliases.
312

369	Schema digest contract:
370	
371	- `input_schema_digest` and `output_schema_digest` are lowercase hex `sha256` digests over the JSON Schema subtree only.
372	- The bytes hashed are RFC 8785 JCS-canonicalized JSON. Object keys are sorted by the canonicalization algorithm, number/string escaping follows JCS, and `$ref` values are hashed literally rather than resolved during digesting.
373	- The digest excludes surrounding manifest metadata such as `ToolID`, handler, source, risk, and toolsets.
374	- TypeScript SDK, Go SDK, and daemon manifest validation must share byte-vector fixtures under `sdk/typescript/test-fixtures/digest/`, `sdk/go/test-fixtures/digest/`, and `internal/extension/testdata/digest/`.
375	- A digest mismatch is a hard `extension_runtime_mismatch`. There is no loose fallback, serializer-specific fallback, or warning-only mode.
376	
377	`MCPToolDescriptor`
378	
379	- `raw_name string`
380	- `title string`
381	- `description string`
382	- `input_schema json.RawMessage`
383	- `output_schema json.RawMessage`
384	- `source SourceRef`
385	
386	`MCPToolDescriptor` is the daemon-internal normalized projection of one upstream MCP `Tool` before registry `Descriptor` synthesis. Hosted MCP descriptors preserve exact descriptor-authored schema bytes through `RawInputSchema` and `RawOutputSchema`. For external MCP discovery, AGH preserves raw schema bytes when the library surfaces them directly; otherwise it stores one canonical JSON encoding of the decoded schema and treats that canonical blob as authoritative for downstream digesting. AGH does not invent missing schemas or use reflection helpers during MCP normalization.

437	MCP-backed descriptors may attach a redacted `MCPAuthStatus` to operator-visible tool views only. The status mirrors the existing `internal/mcp/auth.StatusValue` values (`unconfigured`, `needs_login`, `authenticated`, `expired`, `invalid`) plus registry reason codes, and may include `server_name`, `auth_type`, `client_id`, `scopes`, `expires_at`, `refreshable`, `token_present`, and `diagnostic`. It must never include access tokens, refresh tokens, OAuth authorization codes, PKCE verifiers, client secrets, approval tokens, or hosted MCP bind nonces. Session/model-visible projections do not include `MCPAuthStatus`; they hide or deny the tool through `Availability` reason codes.

466	`Toolset`
467	
468	Toolsets are separate named resources/config entries, not overloaded as tools. They use a typed `ToolsetID`, include exact `ToolID`s and patterns, and may include other toolsets recursively. Policy fields must distinguish `tools` and `toolsets` to avoid ambiguity.
469	
470	`ToolsetID` uses the same grammar as `ToolID`: lowercase ASCII segments separated by reserved `__`, maximum 64 characters, and no dots, hyphens, uppercase, or empty segments.

472	`dynamic` source kind is reserved because the existing source enum already has it, but MVP has no dynamic producer and no dynamic validation surface. Dynamic tools remain unavailable unless a later TechSpec defines their producer, policy, and provenance model.

474	### Data-Model Field Rationale
475	
476	| Field or key | Shape | Purpose | Storage decision |
477	|---|---|---|---|
478	| `ToolID` | string, provider-safe `__`-segmented id | Single policy/dispatch/audit identity across registry, CLI, HTTP, UDS, hooks, telemetry, and hosted MCP | Typed field, never inferred from display title |
479	| `Descriptor.backend` | structured `BackendRef` | Binds the descriptor to the only allowed executable backend path | Typed struct; dispatch never infers backend from source prefix or metadata |

481	| `Descriptor.input_schema` | JSON Schema object | Validate call input before dispatch | JSON schema payload is appropriate because schema content is externally structured and opaque to AGH queries |
482	| `Descriptor.output_schema` | JSON Schema object | Optional structured output contract | JSON schema payload is appropriate for the same reason as input schema |
483	| `Descriptor.visibility` | enum | Separate internal/operator/session/model surfaces | Typed enum, queryable and policy-relevant |
484	| `Descriptor.risk` | enum | Classify read/mutate/network/open-world behavior | Typed enum, never free-text metadata |
485	| `Descriptor.read_only` | bool | Drives `approve-reads` and safety checks | Typed bool; misclassification is a security bug |
486	| `Descriptor.destructive` | bool | Forces stricter policy and approval behavior | Typed bool, not metadata |
487	| `Descriptor.open_world` | bool | Marks tools that can reach arbitrary external state | Typed bool, not metadata |

489	| `Descriptor.max_result_bytes` | int64 | Enforces result budget consistently across surfaces | Typed numeric field with config default |

506	No new SQLite columns are required for the MVP registry foundation. Existing session lineage stores concrete tool permission atoms; implementation should validate those atoms as canonical `ToolID`s. Existing remote MCP OAuth tokens already live in `globaldb.mcp_auth_tokens` through `mcpauth.TokenStore`; registry work must not add token fields to tool descriptors, resources, events, or metadata. Hosted MCP bind nonces are ephemeral launch correlation values and must not be stored in `mcp_auth_tokens`; if a later design needs durable hosted-proxy credential state, it must add a separate typed table with its own lifecycle and redaction tests. If a later task needs durable queryable tool-call history beyond append-only events, it must add a typed side table such as `tool_calls` rather than placing queryable call state in a session metadata JSON blob.
507	
508	### Side-Table vs JSON Decisions
509	
510	| Domain state | Decision | Rationale |
511	|---|---|---|
512	| Tool descriptors | Typed resource specs plus runtime descriptors | Descriptors are matchable by id/source/risk/visibility and must not live as opaque metadata blobs |
513	| Toolsets | Typed config/resource records | Toolset membership affects policy and lineage; it must be queryable and expandable deterministically |
514	| Tool call events | Append-only event payloads for MVP; typed side table only if queryable history is required | Events are the operational ledger; indexed call history would be matchable state and must not be hidden in JSON metadata |
515	| Source provenance | Structured `SourceRef` fields | Raw MCP/extension names are needed for debugging and collision handling |
516	| Input/output schemas | JSON Schema blobs | Schema contents are inherently opaque external contracts and are not AGH ownership state |
517	| Tool result structured payload | JSON payload plus typed envelope fields | Result body can be arbitrary, but status, bytes, truncation, redaction, and tool id are typed envelope fields |
518	| Policy decisions | Typed `EffectiveToolDecision` | Authorization is matchable and auditable; it cannot be a JSON bag |
519	| Availability reasons | Typed reason-code list | Operator diagnostics and tests need deterministic matching |

584	## Integration Points
585	
586	### ACP
587	
588	ACP does not impose a callable tool registry pattern. AGH must not use ACP `ToolCall.title` as a policy identity or dispatch key.
589	
590	AGH will integrate with ACP by:
591	
592	- passing the hosted AGH MCP server in ACP `mcpServers` during session creation/load when the selected agent supports MCP;
593	- preserving ACP tool-call observations as session events keyed by `toolCallId`;
594	- mapping AGH-owned tool calls back into ACP lifecycle updates where relevant;
595	- treating ACP `ToolKind` as risk/display metadata, not identity;
596	- keeping `permissions.mode` as the system/session approval ceiling.
597	
598	Current-state caveat: `internal/acp.toSDKMCPServers` currently emits stdio-only `acpsdk.McpServer` values. MVP registry work must keep hosted AGH MCP as a stdio-only injected server and must not imply remote MCP HTTP ACP parity until a later implementation adds tested HTTP conversion, redacted Authorization/header handling, and provider capability checks.
599	
600	### Hosted MCP
601	
602	MVP exposure path:
603	
604	```text
605	agent session -> ACP mcpServers -> agh tool mcp --session <id> --bind-nonce <nonce> -> UDS -> daemon Tool Registry
606	```
607	
608	The hosted MCP server lists only session-callable tools. It exposes MCP `Tool.name` equal to AGH canonical `ToolID`. It does not expose unavailable, unauthorized, or conflicted tools to the model-visible surface.
609	
610	The proxy process is an `mcp-go`-based stdio MCP server: `agh tool mcp` constructs a `server.NewMCPServer(...)`, registers one `mcp.Tool` per session-callable registry descriptor using the descriptor's exact schema bytes through `RawInputSchema` and `RawOutputSchema`, and serves over `server.ServeStdio`. The UDS bind, session/workspace scoping, approval bridge, result limiting, and dispatch callback remain AGH-owned.
611	
612	Hosted MCP authentication:
613	
614	- On session creation/load, the daemon records a short-lived hosted MCP launch record keyed by `session_id`, `workspace_id`, an opaque non-secret `hosted_mcp_bind_nonce`, expiry, and expected AGH binary path.
615	- The `hosted_mcp_bind_nonce` is a correlation nonce, not a bearer secret and not claim-token-equivalent. It may traverse ACP `mcpServers[].args` because it is insufficient without UDS peer credentials; raw `claim_token`, remote MCP OAuth tokens, and approval tokens never traverse this path.
616	- At startup, `agh tool mcp --session <id> --bind-nonce <nonce>` performs a UDS bind RPC. The daemon accepts the bind only when the nonce matches a live launch record, the Unix-domain socket peer credentials identify the same OS user, the peer executable matches the expected AGH binary, and the record has not expired.
617	- If the platform cannot provide peer credentials or executable validation, hosted MCP binding fails closed and the session receives no hosted registry projection on that platform.
618	- The daemon binds the UDS connection to exactly one session/workspace projection and rejects any later client-supplied `session_id` or `workspace_id`.
619	- The launch record is invalidated on first successful bind, session end, proxy disconnect, or TTL expiry, whichever happens first.
620	- A foreign process calling `agh tool mcp --session <id>` without a valid nonce plus matching UDS peer credentials receives a deterministic permission error and no tool projection.
621	- Redaction tests must cover ACP payload diagnostics, process diagnostics, crash bundles, logs, SSE/events, settings output, tool results, and MCP responses. The nonce may appear in AGH-owned diagnostics as a redacted/correlatable launch id, but it must never be described as a claim-token-equivalent bearer secret.
622	
623	Hosted MCP approval bridge:
624	
625	- Hosted MCP projections include only tools that are callable without a new approval prompt or tools whose session has a live daemon-mediated approval channel.
626	- When `EffectiveToolDecision.approval_required=true` and ACP `session/request_permission` is available, `Registry.Call` derives a context with `[tools.policy].approval_timeout_seconds`, issues the ACP permission request, and blocks the MCP `tools/call` response until approved, denied, timed out, canceled, or the hosted MCP stdio/UDS connection closes.
627	- Hosted MCP request contexts must reserve a guard band above approval waiting. The library-facing hosted MCP request deadline must be at least `[tools.policy].approval_timeout_seconds + 5s` so a valid daemon approval bridge wait cannot be preempted by an earlier transport deadline.
628	- When no approval channel is available, hosted MCP hides the tool from `tools/list` if that can be determined during projection. If a call still reaches dispatch, it returns `ErrToolApprovalRequired` with reason codes `approval_required` and `approval_unreachable`.
629	- Approval timeout returns `ErrToolApprovalRequired` with `approval_required` and `approval_timed_out`. Hosted MCP proxy disconnect or stdio close cancels the derived context and returns `approval_required` plus `approval_canceled`.
630	- Hosted MCP cannot satisfy approval using client-supplied arguments. CLI/HTTP/UDS may use `approval_token`; hosted MCP must use the daemon approval bridge.
631	- Remote MCP outbound call deadlines are derived independently from backend call policy and must not inherit the hosted MCP approval-wait deadline after approval completes.
632	
633	Hosted MCP lifecycle:
634	
635	- The stdio proxy is spawned by the ACP runtime from AGH-provided `mcpServers` config and is scoped to one AGH session.
636	- After successful bind, the proxy opens a daemon-owned UDS projection stream for that bound session. The daemon pushes add/remove/update deltas for session-callable tools; the proxy translates them into `mcp-go` add/remove/replace operations so the library emits `notifications/tools/list_changed` and `tools/list` stays equal to the daemon projection.
637	- If the projection stream drops, the proxy must fail closed by closing the MCP session instead of continuing to serve a stale tool catalog.
638	- The proxy exits when stdio closes, when the session stops, or when the launch record expires before successful bind.
639	- On ACP `session/load`, the daemon mints a fresh bind nonce and provides a fresh hosted MCP entry for that resumed session.
640	- The proxy never accepts a client-supplied workspace id. The daemon derives workspace id from the bound session at projection time and dispatch time.
641	
642	### Existing MCP Config And Auth
643	
644	AGH already has an MCP server configuration and remote-auth subsystem. The Tool Registry must consume those surfaces instead of defining a parallel MCP model:
645	
646	- `internal/config/provider.go` currently defines `MCPServer`, `MCPServerTransport` (`stdio`, `http`, `sse`), and `MCPAuthConfig` for OAuth 2.1 + PKCE metadata/client settings. Token material is explicitly outside config. This TechSpec keeps that remote transport surface and maps it directly onto `mcp-go` client transports.
647	- `internal/config/mcpjson.go` loads `mcp.json` sidecars using `mcpServers` or `mcp_servers`.
648	- `internal/config/mcp_resource.go` validates `mcp_server` desired-state resources.
649	- `internal/mcp/auth` owns metadata discovery, PKCE state, authorization-code exchange, refresh, redacted status, logout/revocation, and `StatusValue`.
650	- `internal/store/globaldb/global_db_mcp_auth.go` persists remote MCP OAuth tokens behind `mcpauth.TokenStore` with encryption/redaction boundaries.
651	- `internal/cli/mcp_auth.go` provides the existing agent-operable commands `agh mcp auth login`, `agh mcp auth status`, and `agh mcp auth logout`.
652	- `internal/settings` and `internal/api/contract/settings.go` already expose redacted MCP auth status for settings surfaces.
653	
654	The hosted MCP bind nonce described above is not a remote MCP OAuth token and is not a bearer credential by itself. It is a daemon-minted correlation value for AGH's local stdio proxy, validated together with UDS peer credentials and the expected AGH binary path. Remote MCP OAuth tokens remain owned by `internal/mcp/auth` and `globaldb`. Registry descriptors, events, tool results, and MCP proxy arguments must never mix those credentials or reuse one lifecycle for the other.
655	
656	Remote MCP call-through uses an `MCPCallExecutor` implemented inside `internal/mcp`. `internal/tools` depends only on the executor interface and redacted `MCPAuthStatusProvider`; it must not import `internal/mcp/auth`, open `mcpauth.TokenStore`, receive raw bearer strings, or construct Authorization headers. The executor resolves bearer material internally, applies transport-specific headers in memory for the outbound MCP request, and maps failures back to redacted registry errors.
657	
658	Implementation direction for the executor:
659	
660	- stdio MCP servers use `client.NewStdioMCPClient`;
661	- remote HTTP MCP servers use `client.NewStreamableHttpClient`;
662	- remote SSE MCP servers use `client.NewSSEMCPClient`;
663	- `MCPCallExecutor` maintains one `mcp-go` client/session per `SourceRef` with a bounded idle TTL, recreates it after transport failure or auth-status change, and applies registry-derived per-call deadlines to `ListTools` and `CallTool`;
664	- for remote HTTP and SSE, outbound auth headers are injected from current AGH-owned credential state using library header hooks inside `internal/mcp`;
665	- the executor may attempt at most one `internal/mcp/auth.Service.Refresh` for refreshable `authenticated` or `expired` states before client creation or one retry after an auth failure; it must never bootstrap a new login flow and must return only redacted reason-mapped errors otherwise;
666	- AGH must not instantiate `client.NewOAuthStreamableHttpClient`, `client.NewOAuthSSEClient`, default `transport.NewOAuthHandler`, `MemoryTokenStore`, or any other library-managed OAuth flow as the authority for remote MCP credentials;
667	- MVP external MCP descriptor freshness is on-demand, not subscription-based: the daemon refreshes upstream MCP descriptors during projection rebuilds and before a call when the cached descriptor set is missing or stale. It does not rely on long-lived upstream `notifications/tools/list_changed` subscriptions from external MCP servers in MVP.
668	- If an upstream external MCP server still delivers `notifications/tools/list_changed` during an active client session, AGH treats it only as a local cache-invalidation hint. It must not mutate registry structure from the notification alone and must not maintain standalone notification subscriptions in MVP.
669	- no new manual MCP message framing, request/response routing, or transport-specific retry layer should be introduced outside the `mcp-go` wrapper without a later ADR.
670	
671	External MCP-backed tool availability must derive auth diagnostics from the existing auth service:
672	
673	| `internal/mcp/auth.StatusValue` | Registry reason code | Session projection behavior |
674	|---|---|---|
675	| `unconfigured` | `mcp_auth_unconfigured` | Hide external MCP tools unless the server is public and executable support exists |
676	| `needs_login` | `mcp_auth_required` | Hide from model-visible projection; operator view points to `agh mcp auth login <server>` |
677	| `authenticated` | none | Auth does not block availability; calls may proceed only through the daemon-owned MCP adapter after registry policy passes |
678	| `expired` | `mcp_auth_expired` | Hide from model-visible projection; operator view points to `agh mcp auth status --refresh <server>` |
679	| `invalid` | `mcp_auth_invalid` | Hide from model-visible projection; operator view points to logout/login repair |
680	
681	Implementation correction required before the registry consumes MCP resource catalogs: current `internal/daemon/tool_mcp_resources.go` clones MCP server records through `cloneDaemonMCPServer`, which preserves only `Name`, `Command`, `Args`, and `Env`. Registry work that depends on remote MCP resources must update that clone path and its tests to preserve `Transport`, `URL`, and `Auth`; otherwise remote MCP auth metadata will be silently dropped from tool diagnostics.

683	### Extensions
684	
685	Extension-installed tools are possible and should be first-class.
686	
687	Current foundation already has:
688	
689	- `extension.toml` `resources.tools`
690	- `resources.publish.families = ["tools"]`
691	- resource projection into daemon tool records
692	- extension health/status infrastructure
693	- Host API capability checks
694	
695	This TechSpec extends that by adding manifest-authoritative backend metadata, runtime reconciliation, and executable out-of-process handlers:
696	
697	```toml
698	[resources.tools.search]
699	id = "ext__linear__search"
700	description = "Search Linear issues"
701	read_only = true
702	backend.kind = "extension_host"
703	backend.handler = "search"
704	toolsets = ["linear__read"]
705	```
706	
707	TypeScript extensions define the matching runtime handler through `@agh/extension-sdk`:
708	
709	```ts
710	extension.tool("search", {
711	  readOnly: true,
712	  inputSchema: z.object({ query: z.string() }),
713	}, async ({ input, context }) => {
714	  return { content: [{ type: "text", text: await searchLinear(input.query, context) }] };
715	});
716	```
717	
718	Go extensions define the same handler through the public Go extension SDK:
719	
720	```go
721	ext.Tool("search", aghsdk.ToolOptions{
722		ReadOnly: true,
723		InputSchema: searchInputSchema,
724	}, func(ctx context.Context, req aghsdk.ToolRequest[SearchInput]) (aghsdk.ToolResult, error) {
725		return searchLinear(ctx, req.Input)
726	})
727	```
728	
729	Extensions must not freely impersonate `agh__*` or another extension namespace. Raw manifest names remain in `SourceRef`. `extension.toml` is the source of truth; SDK registration is runtime proof that the live extension process implements the manifest-declared handler and compatible schemas.
730	
731	Execution boundaries:
732	
733	- `native_go`: full MVP dispatch through in-process daemon code compiled with AGH.
734	- `extension_host`: full MVP dispatch through out-of-process extension runtime, `tool.provider`, `provide_tools`, and `tools/call`.
735	- `mcp`: full MVP dispatch through daemon-owned MCP client adapters using existing MCP config/auth.
736	- `subprocess`: not a separate public backend kind; TypeScript and Go extension SDKs use the existing subprocess runtime behind `extension_host`.
737	- `bridge`: future bridge adapter, rejected by MVP validation unless a later TechSpec enables it.
738	
739	No in-process third-party extension handlers in MVP. A Go function authored by an extension runs in the extension's subprocess binary through the Go SDK, not inside the daemon.
740	
741	### MCP Sources
742	
743	MCP-backed tools use:
744	
745	```text
746	mcp__<server>__<tool>
747	```
748	
749	The registry must preserve raw server/tool names in `SourceRef`. Sanitization collisions fail closed and mark the candidate tool `conflicted`.
750	
751	Canonicalization contract:
752	
753	```go
754	func Canonicalize(rawServer, rawTool string) (ToolID, error)
755	```
756	
757	Rules:
758	
759	- trim surrounding ASCII whitespace from `rawServer` and `rawTool`; either empty result is invalid;
760	- lowercase ASCII letters;
761	- replace `-` and `.` with `_` inside each raw segment;
762	- reject any rune outside ASCII letters, digits, `_`, `-`, and `.` rather than transliterating or dropping it;
763	- reject any normalized segment that starts with a digit, becomes empty, or contains `__` after normalization;
764	- reject any normalized segment whose leading/trailing `_` would make the reserved `__` separator ambiguous;
765	- assemble the result as `mcp__<server>__<tool>`;
766	- if the assembled ID exceeds 64 characters, fail with `id_too_long`;
767	- if two distinct raw `(server, tool)` pairs normalize to the same assembled ID, keep raw provenance in `SourceRef` and fail closed with `conflicted_sanitized_name` at registry indexing time.
768	
769	`Canonicalize` is the only allowed raw-name to canonical-`ToolID` transform for MCP sources. `MCPCallExecutor`, MCP descriptor normalization, hosted MCP registration, and config/resource validation must all reference the same helper and share one fixture set proving byte-stable results.
770	
771	AGH-managed MCP sources in MVP are the existing validated projections, not raw file scans:
772	
773	- top-level and workspace MCP config plus global/workspace `mcp.json` sidecars;
774	- provider and agent MCP server declarations;
775	- skill MCP declarations resolved by `internal/skills.MCPResolver`, including the existing `allowed_marketplace_mcp` trust gate;
776	- extension `resources.mcp_servers` records resolved by `internal/extension/resource_publication.go`;
777	- future client-supplied ACP `mcpServers` only if a later TechSpec makes them session-scoped tool sources with explicit source trust and collision handling.
778	
779	Top-level, provider, agent, and `mcp.json` declarations use `aghconfig.MCPServer` and may carry remote `transport`, `url`, and `auth` fields. Current skill and extension MCP declarations are stdio subprocess declarations with `name`, `command`, `args`, and `env`; registry work must not infer remote OAuth support from them until their manifests are explicitly extended.
780	
781	External MCP descriptor discovery must consume existing `aghconfig.MCPServer` config/resource projections and `internal/mcp/auth` redacted status. 1027	### Agent Definitions
1028	
1029	Keep the existing `tools` field but harden its meaning:
1030	
1031	- `tools`: exact canonical `ToolID`s or approved wildcard patterns.
1032	- `toolsets`: named toolset IDs.
1033	- `deny_tools`: exact IDs or patterns that always narrow permissions.
1034	
1035	Session lineage should persist concrete resolved `ToolID` atoms, not broad unresolved wildcard patterns. Child session permissions must remain subsets of parent session permissions.
1036	
1037	Invalid existing lineage atoms reject session spawn/load with a typed validation error. Greenfield posture applies: AGH does not silently normalize old atoms, and local databases that predate this TechSpec require a fresh `AGH_HOME` rather than compatibility migration shims.
1038	
1039	### Tool Pattern Grammar
1040	
1041	Allowed policy pattern forms:
1042	
1043	- exact canonical IDs, for example `agh__skill_view`;
1044	- namespace-prefix wildcards ending in `*`, for example `agh__skill_*` or `mcp__github__*`;
1045	- toolset IDs in `toolsets`, never in `tools`.
1046	
1047	Disallowed forms:
1048	
1049	- regular expressions;
1050	- suffix wildcards such as `*__search`;
1051	- mid-segment wildcards such as `agh__*__view`;
1052	- uppercase, dots, hyphens, or empty segments;
1053	- wildcard forms that would match across a reserved `__` boundary ambiguously.
1054	
1055	Pattern matching runs against canonical `ToolID` only. Display titles, raw MCP tool names, and extension manifest names do not participate in policy matching.

1058

1060	
1061	- invalid `ToolID` patterns;
1062	- unknown toolset IDs when a config is resolved in a concrete workspace;

1065	- legacy external MCP transport value `sse`; use `http` or fail validation;
1066	- global defaults that would expose external tools without source policy support;
1067	- `trusted_sources` entries that do not resolve to known extension/MCP source refs;

1179	- Hooks can deny pre-call and redact post-call output.
1180	- Conflicted tool IDs are operator-visible and session-hidden.
1181	- Result budget truncation is identical across CLI, HTTP, UDS, and MCP.

1184	
1185	Use the AGH runtime E2E harness:

1202	## Implementation Steps
1203	
1204	### Build Order
1205	
1206	Ordered implementation sequence respecting dependencies:
1207	
1208	1. Add canonical `ToolID`, descriptor, backend kind, source, availability, result, and error contracts in `internal/tools` - no dependencies.
1209	2. Replace metadata-only provider semantics with executable runtime provider/handle interfaces - depends on step 1.
1210	3. Implement registry indexing, collision detection, MCP auth diagnostic mapping, and operator/session projections - depends on steps 1-2 and existing `internal/mcp/auth`.
1211	4. Implement policy evaluator with ACP ceiling, agent policy, session lineage, source defaults, allow/deny, and toolsets - depends on step 3.
1212	5. Implement dispatch pipeline with schema validation, availability recheck, hooks, budgets, handle call, normalization, and telemetry seams - depends on steps 3-4.
1213	6. Add built-in provider for bootstrap AGH tools: `agh__tool_*`, `agh__skill_*`, `agh__network_*`, and only the enumerated MVP task tools - depends on step 5.
1214	7. Add extension manifest backend metadata, manifest-authoritative validation, and runtime reconciliation contracts - depends on steps 1-3 and existing extension resources.
1215	8. Add extension protocol capability `tool.provider`, wire-stable `provide_tools`/`tools/call` request-response structs, schema digest conformance fixtures, and invocation through the existing subprocess manager - depends on steps 5 and 7.
1216	9. Add TypeScript SDK `extension.tool(...)`, schema digesting, and handler registration - depends on step 8.
1217	10. Add public Go extension SDK with function-based tool helpers equivalent to TypeScript SDK - depends on step 8.
1218	11. Add daemon-owned MCP descriptor discovery and `MCPCallExecutor` call-through adapter using existing MCP config/auth, token redaction boundaries, `mcp-go` version `v0.49.0`, canonical `Canonicalize(rawServer, rawTool)` normalization, and explicit stdio/streamable-HTTP/SSE client transports - depends on steps 3-5 and existing `internal/mcp/auth`.
1219	12. Add hosted MCP stdio proxy command `agh tool mcp --session --bind-nonce`, implemented with `mcp-go` server/tool APIs over `server.ServeStdio`, exact descriptor schema bytes through `RawInputSchema`/`RawOutputSchema`, a daemon→proxy projection stream, and UDS peer-credential bind plus approval bridge timeout/cancellation and existing MCP resource/auth preservation - depends on steps 3-6 and 11.
1220	13. Add API contract DTOs and `internal/api/core` handlers - depends on steps 3-6, 8, and 11.
1221	14. Wire HTTP, UDS, CLI commands, and UDS client methods - depends on step 13.
1222	15. Wire hooks and canonical `tool_id` payload updates end-to-end, including typed hook payloads, matchers, fixture builders, extension-author docs, and no dual identity mid-PR - depends on step 5.
1223	16. Add config lifecycle, validation, generated docs, CLI docs, site docs, integration/E2E coverage, and run `make verify` - depends on all prior steps.
1224	
1225	### Technical Dependencies
1226	
1227	Blocking dependencies that must be resolved before implementation:
1228	
1229	- Existing ACP `permissions.mode` behavior in `internal/acp/permission.go`.
1230	- Existing session lineage permission atoms in `internal/store/session_lineage.go`.
1231	- Existing extension resource publication and `resources.publish.families = ["tools"]`.
1232	- Existing extension subprocess lifecycle, JSON-RPC `process.Call`, Host API capability checks, and TypeScript `Extension.handle(...)` handler pattern.
1233	- Existing hooks payload system.
1234	- Existing MCP server configuration/resource lifecycle in `internal/config/provider.go`, `internal/config/mcpjson.go`, `internal/config/mcp_resource.go`, `internal/skills/mcp.go`, and `internal/extension/resource_publication.go`.
1235	- Existing MCP auth lifecycle in `internal/mcp/auth`, `internal/store/globaldb/global_db_mcp_auth.go`, `internal/cli/mcp_auth.go`, `internal/settings`, and `internal/daemon/settings.go`.
1236	- Existing skills registry APIs.
1237	- Existing task/network services.
1238	- OpenAPI/codegen path for web contract updates.
1239	
1240	### Safety Invariants
1241	
1242	1. Every AGH-owned tool call enters `internal/tools.Registry.Call`; CLI, HTTP, UDS, hosted MCP, extension, and session paths cannot bypass the registry dispatch pipeline.
1243	2. Dispatch recomputes availability and `EffectiveToolDecision` at call time, even when discovery already filtered the tool.
1244	3. ACP `permissions.mode` is always a ceiling. Registry, source, agent, session, and hook policy can narrow authority but cannot raise it above the effective ACP/session mode.
1245	4. `approve-all` skips approval prompts only for otherwise allowed tools; it does not bypass explicit denies, source grants, session lineage, conflicts, unavailable backends, or hooks.
1246	5. `approve-reads` applies only to registry-classified read-only tools. Extension/MCP read-only tools also require an explicit trusted source or per-tool/toolset/source/agent grant. Mutating, destructive, open-world, network-send, and task-write tools cannot inherit read approval by display title or ACP kind.
1247	6. Session lineage stores concrete canonical `ToolID` atoms after toolset expansion; child sessions can only receive a subset of parent concrete atoms.
1248	7. Tool ID collisions fail closed. A conflicted tool is operator-visible with reason codes and absent from session/model-visible projections.
1249	8. Extension-installed tools can become executable in MVP only when the manifest-authoritative descriptor, source policy, extension health, runtime `provide_tools` descriptor, and `tools/call` handler all agree.
1250	9. Third-party extension tool handlers never run in-process in the daemon during MVP; TypeScript and Go function handlers run inside supervised extension subprocesses.
1251	10. Hooks dispatch at the call site and cannot tail event tables, spawn parallel queues, or mutate durable ownership state outside typed hook contracts.
1252	11. Tool result limiting and redaction run before results cross CLI, HTTP, UDS, MCP, SSE, logs, memory, or event payloads.
1253	12. Raw `claim_token`, MCP auth tokens, OAuth codes, PKCE verifiers, and secret bindings never appear in tool inputs/outputs persisted or emitted by AGH-owned surfaces.
1254	13. Hosted MCP `tools/list` is a projection of `GET /api/sessions/{id}/tools`; divergence is a test failure.
1255	14. Operator-visible diagnostics are not security boundaries. Hidden session projection plus dispatch-time revalidation is the security boundary.
1256	15. Toolsets are expanded deterministically and cycle-checked before session projection; recursive expansion cannot happen lazily during dispatch.
1257	16. Hosted MCP cannot bind to a session without a daemon-minted, single-use, session-bound bind nonce plus successful UDS peer credential and AGH binary validation. The nonce is not a bearer secret, is never accepted from tool input, and its launch record is invalidated on bind/session end/disconnect or `[tools.hosted_mcp].bind_nonce_ttl_seconds`.
1258	17. Hosted MCP `tools/call` cannot pass an `approval_required` decision unless the daemon completes an ACP/session-mediated approval equivalent to CLI/HTTP/UDS approval semantics within `[tools.policy].approval_timeout_seconds`; timeout and proxy disconnect return `approval_timed_out` or `approval_canceled`.
1259	18. No `agh__task_*` tool may bypass `task.Service.ClaimNextRun`, `Spawn`, session manager terminal-state authority, or task lifecycle authority. Claim/release/complete/fail/run-start operations are excluded from MVP tools.
1260	19. External `extension_host` and `mcp` backend tools are executable only through their registered runtime handles; missing handlers, missing MCP clients, missing capabilities, source denies, auth failures, or runtime mismatches fail closed before user code or remote tools run.
1261	20. Remote MCP OAuth/PKCE credentials are owned only by `internal/mcp/auth` and its `TokenStore`; the registry may consume redacted status and call through a narrow `internal/mcp/auth`-owned interface, but cannot persist, log, refresh, revoke, or copy access/refresh tokens. Raw tokens stay out of descriptors, resources, events, API responses, CLI output, MCP responses, and tool results.
1262	21. Hosted MCP bind nonces and remote MCP OAuth tokens have separate issuers, storage, lifetimes, redaction labels, and failure codes. A `hosted_mcp_bind_nonce` is not sufficient to bind an AGH hosted MCP proxy without UDS peer credential validation, must never satisfy a remote MCP server auth check, and a remote MCP OAuth token must never bind an AGH hosted MCP proxy.
1263	22. Hosted MCP `mcp.Tool` registration uses `Descriptor.input_schema` and `Descriptor.output_schema` bytes as authoritative through `RawInputSchema` and `RawOutputSchema`. `WithInputSchema`, `WithOutputSchema`, and reflection-based schema generation helpers are forbidden for AGH-hosted tools.
1264	23. AGH must not use `client.NewOAuthStreamableHttpClient`, `client.NewOAuthSSEClient`, default `transport.NewOAuthHandler`, `MemoryTokenStore`, or any library-owned token cache/login/refresh flow for remote MCP credentials. Remote auth remains `internal/mcp/auth` owned, outbound headers are injected only inside `internal/mcp`, and retry logic may attempt at most one `internal/mcp/auth.Service.Refresh`; it must never start a new login flow.
1265	24. Hosted MCP must maintain a live daemon→proxy projection stream after bind. If the stream fails, the proxy closes the MCP session rather than serving stale tools.
1266	25. AGH-owned approval timeouts govern hosted MCP approval waits; library-side request deadlines must not preempt a still-valid daemon approval bridge wait. Hosted MCP request deadlines therefore include at least a 5-second guard band above `[tools.policy].approval_timeout_seconds`, while remote MCP outbound deadlines are derived independently.
1267	26. Post-change remote MCP config accepts `stdio`, `http`, and `sse`. `http` maps to the explicit `mcp-go` streamable HTTP client path, `sse` maps to the explicit `mcp-go` SSE client path, and neither transport may be silently rewritten into the other.

1327	
1328	## Technical Considerations
1329	
1330	### Key Decisions
1331	
1332	Decision: Tool Registry is an AGH daemon runtime service, not an ACP registry.
1333	
1334	Rationale: ACP has no callable tool registry and relies on MCP for tool discovery.
1335	
1336	Trade-off: AGH must maintain its own registry semantics and expose them through MCP/session surfaces.
1337	
1338	Decision: Use one canonical `ToolID` with `__` namespace separators.
1339	
1340	Rationale: Avoid dotted/internal plus wire alias ambiguity and stay compatible with stricter provider naming limits.
1341	
1342	Trade-off: `agh__skill_view` is less visually elegant than `agh.skill.view`, but it prevents dual identity bugs.
1343	
1344	Decision: Built-ins execute in-process; TypeScript and Go extension tools execute out-of-process through `extension_host`.
1345	
1346	Rationale: Daemon safety and extension isolation matter more than plugin convenience.
1347	
1348	Trade-off: Extension tool latency and setup are higher, but failure containment is better and extension authors still get function-based APIs.
1349	
1350	Decision: Remote MCP-backed tools execute in MVP through daemon-owned MCP clients.
1351	
1352	Rationale: MCP config/auth already exists and the registry must not become useful only for descriptors.
1353	
1354	Trade-off: The MVP must implement stricter auth redaction and adapter tests, but operators get one coherent tool model.
1355	
1356	Decision: ACP `permissions.mode` is the approval ceiling.
1357	
1358	Rationale: Avoid contradictory policy layers and keep existing settings truthful.
1359	
1360	Trade-off: Registry policy must compute effective decisions rather than simple allow/deny flags.
1361	
1362	Decision: Operator and session projections differ.
1363	
1364	Rationale: Operators need diagnostics; models should see only callable tools.
1365	
1366	Trade-off: More projection logic, but less model confusion and fewer unsafe calls.
1367	
1368	Decision: Keep cold resource records separate from runtime handles.
1369	
1370	Rationale: Resource/projector system is good for installed metadata; executable dispatch needs function/protocol handles and live health.
1371	
1372	Trade-off: More types, but cleaner lifecycle and safer extension support.
1373	
1374	### Known Risks
1375	
1376	Risk: Mutating tools mislabeled as read-only.
1377	
1378	Mitigation: Descriptor validation, review tests, policy matrix tests, and hook/audit visibility.
1379	
1380	Risk: External tool name collisions force late breaking changes.
1381	
1382	Mitigation: Enforce canonical `ToolID` grammar and fail-closed collision handling in MVP.
1383	
1384	Risk: Hosted MCP path diverges from CLI/HTTP/UDS behavior.
1385	
1386	Mitigation: Hosted MCP proxies through UDS into the same registry dispatch path.
1387	
1388	Risk: Extension tools become visible before safe runtime execution is confirmed.
1389	
1390	Mitigation: Operator-visible only until manifest/runtime reconciliation, availability, source policy, and backend handle all pass.
1391	
1392	Risk: `approve-all` is misunderstood as "execute everything."
1393	
1394	Mitigation: Docs and UI copy must clarify it auto-approves otherwise allowed calls; it does not bypass explicit denies, source grants, availability, lineage, or hooks.
1395	
1396	Risk: Toolsets create ambiguous policy language.
1397	
1398	Mitigation: Keep `tools` and `toolsets` as separate typed fields; expand toolsets to concrete `ToolID`s for session lineage.
1399	
1400	Risk: Result payloads leak secrets or overwhelm context.
1401	
1402	Mitigation: Central result limiter, redaction metadata, digesting, and output budgets.
1403	
1404	### Delete Targets
1405	
1406	Because AGH is greenfield alpha, the implementation should hard-cut ambiguous old tool concepts instead of adding compatibility bridges:
1407	
1408	- Replace metadata-only `ToolProvider.Tools(ctx)` as the runtime extension point with provider/descriptor/handle contracts.
1409	- Remove descriptor-only MVP wording and `backend_not_implemented` behavior for `extension_host` and `mcp` tools.
1410	- Remove any public standalone `subprocess` backend in favor of `extension_host` subprocess isolation.
1411	- Replace `internal/tools.Tool.Name` as a registry identity with canonical `ToolID` in new public contracts.
1412	- Remove any new public use of dotted tool IDs or separate MCP wire aliases.
1413	- Remove any new forced hard-cut of `mcp_server.transport = "sse"` or any compatibility shim that silently rewrites `sse` to `http`; the runtime must either support the declared transport explicitly or fail with a typed configuration error.
1414	- Delete any new hand-rolled MCP protocol/session/transport implementation for hosted MCP or remote MCP call-through in favor of the `mark3labs/mcp-go` wrapper inside `internal/mcp`, including locally-authored MCP message structs, ad-hoc stdio framers, or custom bearer-header injectors outside `internal/mcp/auth`.
1415	- Rewrite `internal/daemon/tool_mcp_resources.go:cloneDaemonMCPServer` so registry/resource projections preserve `Transport`, `URL`, and `Auth` instead of leaving a partial MCP clone path in tree.
1416	- Replace hook policy identity based on `tool_name` + `tool_namespace` with canonical `tool_id` for registry-owned tool calls, including `internal/hooks/payloads.go` `ToolPreCallPayload`, `ToolPostCallPayload`, and `ToolPostErrorPayload`.
1417	- Update docs, tests, CLI/API examples, and task artifacts that refer to dotted IDs or dual aliasing.
1418	
1419	## Architecture Decision Records
1420	
1421	- [ADR-001: Extension Tool Execution Boundary](adrs/adr-001-extension-tool-execution-boundary.md) - extension tools are manifest-first, executable, and out-of-process in MVP.
1422	- [ADR-002: Session Tool Exposure Path](adrs/adr-002-session-tool-exposure-path.md) - expose AGH registry tools through hosted local MCP plus shared CLI/HTTP/UDS.
1423	- [ADR-003: Runtime Registry Package Boundary](adrs/adr-003-runtime-registry-package-boundary.md) - `internal/tools` owns runtime registry and dispatch; `internal/catalog` remains thin.
1424	- [ADR-004: MVP Native Tool Scope](adrs/adr-004-mvp-native-tool-scope.md) - bootstrap catalog/skill tools plus selected network/task tools.
1425	- [ADR-005: ACP Approval Policy Integration](adrs/adr-005-acp-approval-policy-integration.md) - ACP approval mode is the system/session ceiling.
1426	- [ADR-006: Tool Visibility By Surface](adrs/adr-006-tool-visibility-by-surface.md) - operator projections show diagnostics; model projections show callable tools only.
1427	- [ADR-007: Canonical Tool ID Format](adrs/adr-007-canonical-tool-id-format.md) - one provider-safe `ToolID` using reserved `__` namespace separators.
1428	- [ADR-008: Manifest-Authoritative Extension Tool Descriptors](adrs/adr-008-manifest-authoritative-extension-tool-descriptors.md) - `extension.toml` is source of truth and runtime descriptors reconcile against it.
1429	- [ADR-009: Public Go Extension Tool SDK](adrs/adr-009-public-go-extension-tool-sdk.md) - Go extensions get function-based subprocess SDK APIs equivalent to TypeScript.
1430	- [ADR-010: Remote MCP Call-Through](adrs/adr-010-remote-mcp-call-through.md) - remote MCP tools are executable in MVP through daemon-owned MCP adapters.
1431	- [ADR-011: Use `mark3labs/mcp-go` For MCP Protocol And Transport](adrs/adr-011-mark3labs-mcp-go.md) - hosted MCP and remote MCP call-through use `mcp-go` instead of hand-rolled protocol plumbing.
1432

1451	- `B-002` Schema digest canonicalization: addressed in Data Models with RFC 8785 JCS canonicalization, lowercase SHA-256 digests, and shared SDK/daemon fixtures.

1	# ADR-010: Remote MCP Call-Through In MVP
2	
3	## Status
4	
5	Accepted
6	
7	## Date
8	
9	2026-04-28
10	
11	## Context
12	
13	AGH already models MCP server configuration and remote auth. The previous Tool Registry spec treated MCP-backed tools as descriptors with availability diagnostics only. The revised MVP must make MCP tools executable through the same registry dispatch path as built-in and extension-host tools.
14	
15	Remote MCP call-through must not duplicate AGH's MCP auth model or leak OAuth tokens. ACP currently converts MCP servers to stdio-only session entries, so remote MCP execution should happen inside the daemon-owned registry adapter rather than by passing remote MCP config directly through ACP.
16	
17	## Decision
18	
19	Remote/local MCP tools are executable in the Tool Registry MVP.
20	
21	The daemon owns MCP client adapters that discover/list/call MCP tools from validated MCP configuration and resource sources. The adapters consume existing MCP config, transport, auth metadata, and redacted auth status from `internal/mcp/auth`. Token material remains owned by `internal/mcp/auth` and its `TokenStore`; registry descriptors and results never copy tokens. In this workstream, remote MCP transport support preserves `stdio`, streamable `http`, and declarative `sse`, because `mark3labs/mcp-go` supports all three directly.
22	
23	The call-through contract is an `MCPCallExecutor` implemented inside `internal/mcp`. `internal/tools` may depend on that interface, but it must not import `internal/mcp/auth`, open the token store, receive raw bearer strings, or construct Authorization headers. The executor resolves bearer material internally for each outbound request and returns only normalized results plus redacted errors.
24	
25	Hosted MCP remains AGH's session exposure transport. When an agent calls `mcp__...` through the hosted MCP server, the call re-enters `internal/tools.Registry.Call`; the registry then invokes the daemon-owned MCP client adapter after policy, auth, availability, hook, schema, and result-budget checks.
26	
27	## Alternatives Considered
28	
29	### Descriptor-only MCP tools
30	
31	- **Description**: Show MCP tool descriptors and auth diagnostics, but do not call them in MVP.
32	- **Pros**: Smaller security and transport surface.
33	- **Cons**: Leaves MCP as a second-class source and fails to prove external tool execution.
34	- **Why rejected**: The accepted MVP scope includes remote MCP call-through.
35	
36	### Pass remote MCP servers directly to ACP sessions
37	
38	- **Description**: Let providers connect directly to remote MCP servers.
39	- **Pros**: Less daemon adapter work.
40	- **Cons**: ACP conversion is currently stdio-only, policy/audit is provider-dependent, and AGH cannot centrally enforce result redaction or source grants.
41	- **Why rejected**: AGH needs one daemon-owned dispatch path.
42	
43	### Duplicate MCP auth in the registry
44	
45	- **Description**: Store MCP tokens or OAuth state with tool descriptors.
46	- **Pros**: Simple adapter lookup.
47	- **Cons**: Duplicates credential ownership and increases leak risk.
48	- **Why rejected**: `internal/mcp/auth` remains the sole credential owner.
49	
50	## Consequences
51	
52	### Positive
53	
54	- MCP tools become agent-callable through the same policy, visibility, hook, telemetry, and hosted MCP surfaces as built-ins and extension-host tools.
55	- Existing MCP auth and settings diagnostics remain authoritative.
56	- AGH can enforce a consistent `ToolID` and collision policy for MCP sources.
57	- AGH no longer needs to hard-cut remote `sse` purely because of the protocol library choice.
58	
59	### Negative
60	
61	- MVP must implement daemon-side MCP discovery/call clients, transport handling, timeout behavior, auth refresh/error mapping, and redaction tests.
62	- MCP adapters add more failure states to availability and session projection.
63	
64	### Risks
65	
66	- OAuth tokens could leak through registry output. Mitigation: registry consumes only redacted status and uses narrow `internal/mcp/auth` execution interfaces for bearer material.
67	- Remote MCP call latency could block hosted MCP responses. Mitigation: explicit timeouts, cancellation, and structured backend failure errors.
68	- External MCP tool names could collide after sanitization. Mitigation: fail-closed conflict handling and operator-visible diagnostics.
69	
70	## Implementation Notes
71	
72	- Reuse `aghconfig.MCPServer`, `internal/config/mcpjson.go`, `internal/config/mcp_resource.go`, skill MCP resolution, extension MCP resources, and `internal/mcp/auth`.
73	- Add `MCPCallExecutor` tests proving bearer headers are injected only inside `internal/mcp` and never cross `internal/tools` logs, errors, events, or results.
74	- Fix resource cloning paths that currently drop `Transport`, `URL`, or `Auth` before relying on remote MCP diagnostics/calls.
75	- Add MCP adapter tests for stdio, HTTP, SSE, auth-required, expired/invalid auth, collision, timeout, cancellation, redaction, and transport-specific config preservation.
76	- Use `client.NewStdioMCPClient`, `client.NewStreamableHttpClient`, and `client.NewSSEMCPClient` from `mark3labs/mcp-go`.
77	- Keep upstream `notifications/tools/list_changed` as cache invalidation hints only in MVP; do not let remote notifications mutate registry state directly.
78	- Hosted MCP never receives remote OAuth tokens; it receives only AGH-hosted session projection entries.
79	
80	## References
81	
82	- `internal/config/provider.go`
83	- `internal/mcp/auth`
84	- `internal/store/globaldb/global_db_mcp_auth.go`
85	- `internal/acp/client.go`
86	- `.compozy/tasks/tools-registry/analysis/analysis_acp_tool_registry_compatibility.md`
87

1	# ADR-011: Use `mark3labs/mcp-go` For MCP Protocol And Transport
2	
3	## Status
4	
5	Accepted
6	
7	## Date
8	
9	2026-04-28
10	
11	## Context
12	
13	The Tool Registry TechSpec currently describes hosted MCP exposure and daemon-owned MCP call-through in a way that leaves room for AGH to implement MCP protocol/session/transport behavior manually.
14	
15	That would duplicate a large amount of MCP protocol code that already exists in `github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go`:
16	
17	- client/server lifecycle
18	- stdio transport
19	- streamable HTTP transport
20	- SSE transport
21	- tool registration
22	- `tools/list_changed`
23	- session-scoped tools/filtering
24	- raw input/output schema fields
25	
26	At the same time, AGH already owns product-specific MCP boundaries that the library should not replace:
27	
28	- `internal/mcp/auth` durable token storage and redacted status
29	- CLI/settings/API operator surfaces
30	- registry projection, policy, approval, lineage, provenance, and redaction
31	- hosted MCP UDS bind rules and session scoping
32	
33	## Decision
34	
35	AGH will use `github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go` as the MCP protocol and transport implementation for the Tool Registry MVP.
36	
37	Adoption boundary:
38	
39	- Hosted AGH MCP proxy uses `server.NewMCPServer`, `mcp.Tool`, and `server.ServeStdio` rather than manual MCP message handling.
40	- Daemon-owned MCP call-through uses the library client/transport APIs for stdio, streamable HTTP, and SSE MCP servers.
41	- AGH keeps `internal/mcp/auth` as the durable auth owner. Library OAuth helpers are not the product authority.
42	- AGH keeps registry semantics, policy, approval, projection, redaction, provenance, diagnostics, and canonical `ToolID` semantics outside the library.
43	
44	AGH canonical `ToolID` remains the public identity and is passed to `mcp-go` as MCP `Tool.name`.
45	
46	## Alternatives Considered
47	
48	### Continue with hand-rolled MCP implementation
49	
50	- **Pros**: maximum internal control and no external library dependency.
51	- **Cons**: duplicates already-solved protocol work, increases bug surface for lifecycle/transports/auth wiring, and delays registry delivery.
52	- **Why rejected**: `mcp-go` already covers the protocol layer AGH needs.
53	
54	### Replace AGH auth ownership with library OAuth ownership
55	
56	- **Pros**: more reuse around OAuth.
57	- **Cons**: loses AGH's current durable token store, redacted status model, and operator manageability surfaces.
58	- **Why rejected**: AGH auth is a product boundary, not just a transport detail.
59	
60	### Use the official MCP Go SDK instead of `mcp-go`
61	
62	- **Pros**: tighter governance alignment with the MCP spec source of truth.
63	- **Cons**: for this workstream it forced an unnecessary hard-cut of remote `sse`, gave AGH a less natural auth integration surface, and fit hosted session-scoped tool projection less directly.
64	- **Why rejected**: `mcp-go` matches AGH's practical hosted-proxy and call-through needs better while still preserving AGH-owned policy/auth boundaries.
65	
66	## Consequences
67	
68	### Positive
69	
70	- AGH reuses an existing MCP protocol/session/transport implementation instead of rebuilding it.
71	- Hosted MCP and remote MCP call-through gain first-class stdio, streamable HTTP, and SSE support.
72	- The library's per-session tools and `tools/list_changed` behavior fit AGH's hosted projection model well.
73	- Raw input/output schema fields give AGH a direct way to preserve descriptor-authored hosted schemas.
74	- The Tool Registry TechSpec can focus on AGH-specific semantics instead of rebuilding MCP itself.
75	
76	### Negative
77	
78	- AGH now depends on an evolving external library and must track version compatibility explicitly.
79	- Some AGH requirements still require wrapper code because the library is protocol-level, not product-level.
80	- The library ships OAuth helpers that default to in-memory token storage, which conflicts with AGH's auth ownership if used naively.
81	
82	### Risks
83	
84	- Library behavior changes could leak into AGH MCP behavior.
85	  - Mitigation: pin the module version, wrap usage behind `internal/mcp`, and test hosted/stdio/HTTP/SSE behavior explicitly.
86	- AGH developers may overreach and replace auth/product boundaries with library OAuth defaults.
87	  - Mitigation: keep `internal/mcp/auth` authoritative in the TechSpec and tests, and explicitly forbid `MemoryTokenStore` or library-owned login flows as product authority.
88	- Remote schema fidelity could be overstated if AGH assumes byte-for-byte preservation of schemas discovered from upstream servers.
89	  - Mitigation: keep hosted schemas exact through raw fields, and define external schema preservation in terms of raw bytes when available or canonicalized decoded schema otherwise.
90	
91	## Implementation Notes
92	
93	- Add `github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go` through `go get`; do not edit `go.mod` by hand.
94	- Hosted MCP stdio proxy should use `server.NewMCPServer` plus `server.ServeStdio`, with explicit `mcp.Tool` values carrying descriptor-owned `RawInputSchema` and `RawOutputSchema` bytes.
95	- MCP call-through should use `client.NewStdioMCPClient` for stdio servers, `client.NewStreamableHttpClient` for remote HTTP MCP servers, and `client.NewSSEMCPClient` for remote SSE MCP servers.
96	- AGH keeps the existing `mcp_server.transport = "stdio" | "http" | "sse"` surface. `http` maps to streamable HTTP; `sse` maps to the library's explicit SSE client path.
97	- MVP auth integration should use AGH-owned token state plus header injection or a narrowly-scoped AGH-owned adapter inside `internal/mcp`; it must not rely on `client.NewOAuthStreamableHttpClient`, `client.NewOAuthSSEClient`, default `transport.NewOAuthHandler`, or `MemoryTokenStore` as the product authority.
98	- Any refresh behavior triggered during outbound MCP calls may attempt at most one `internal/mcp/auth.Service.Refresh` and must never start a new login flow outside `agh mcp auth login`.
99	- Upstream `notifications/tools/list_changed` may be used as cache invalidation hints only in MVP; AGH does not let external notifications mutate registry structure directly.
100	
101	## References
102	
103	- `.compozy/tasks/tools-registry/analysis/analysis_mark3labs_mcp_go.md`
104	- `https://github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go`
105	- `https://github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go/releases`
106	- `/Users/pedronauck/go/pkg/mod/github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go@v0.49.0/README.md`
107	- `/Users/pedronauck/go/pkg/mod/github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go@v0.49.0/mcp/tools.go`
108	- `/Users/pedronauck/go/pkg/mod/github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go@v0.49.0/server/server.go`
109	- `/Users/pedronauck/go/pkg/mod/github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go@v0.49.0/server/streamable_http.go`
110	- `/Users/pedronauck/go/pkg/mod/github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go@v0.49.0/client/stdio.go`
111	- `/Users/pedronauck/go/pkg/mod/github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go@v0.49.0/client/http.go`
112	- `/Users/pedronauck/go/pkg/mod/github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go@v0.49.0/client/sse.go`
